title: cephaelis stipulosa
intro: Cephaelis stipulosa, also known as the Madagascar periwinkle or wild periwinkle, is a flowering plant native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Cephaelis stipulosa include its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce fever, and treat various infections. Cephaelis stipulosa can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it medicinally.
